summary: Matched several previously stubbed functions in src/melee/gm/gm_17C0.c, mainly around 1P/adventure-mode setup, trophy unlock state bookkeeping, adventure stage slot lookup, and match-end stock/status handling. The PR also refined related prototypes and struct layouts in gm_17C0.h, gm/types.h, and mn/types.h so argument and callback types better reflect observed usage.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8017DD7C, which populates extra PlayerInitData entries from Unk1PData_x24 character data, setting character kind, slot type, stocks, team, color, CPU level, and several unknown fields, with a special CKIND_GKOOPS bitfield case.
Implemented gm_8017E280 as a small table updater over lbl_804706D8, accumulating unlock-state-like values for a u16 key and returning the stored u8 value or -1 if no slot is available.
Implemented fn_8017E3C8 to iterate lbl_804706D8 and call Trophy_SetUnlockState for valid entries.
Implemented gm_8017E440 to return the current adventure slot unless gm_801677F8 rejects the slot/x4 pair, in which case it returns 4.
Implemented gm_8017E4C4 as a sentinel-terminated lookup over gm_803DE650, returning the matching gm_803DE650_t entry for a u8 key or NULL.
Implemented gm_8017E7A0 to handle arg0 == 1 by calling Player_LoseStock(0), ifStatus_802F6948(0), and ifStatus_802F6E3C(0).
Changed lbl_804706D8_t field layout from two s16 fields to s16 x0 plus u8 x2 and u8 x3, matching the byte-sized counter/state behavior used by gm_8017E280 and fn_8017E3C8.
Added includes for <melee/if/ifstatus.h> and <melee/ty/toy.h> to support newly matched calls.
Updated gm_17C0.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or looser integer types to concrete signatures for fn_8017DD7C, gm_8017E280, fn_8017E3C8, gm_8017E7A0, and gm_8017E7FC.
Expanded Unk1PData_x24 in gm/types.h with byte fields x1, x2, and x3 before existing int fields x4 and x8, enabling typed access in fn_8017DD7C.
Changed StartMeleeRules.x50 callback type in mn/types.h from void (*)(int) to void (*)(u8), aligning the VS match-end callback with gm_8017E7A0/gm_8017E7FC-style byte arguments.
smart_moves: Matched multiple small functions by first tightening the data model: the lbl_804706D8_t split from s16 to u8 fields and Unk1PData_x24 byte-field expansion made the C code express the same access widths as the target.
Used sentinel-based table traversal for gm_8017E4C4, stopping on x0 == 0xFF rather than relying on a hard-coded count when the diff evidence shows the table itself carries its terminator.
Propagated type discoveries into headers and related menu rule callback declarations instead of only forcing local matches in gm_17C0.c.
Preserved unknown field names where semantics were not proven, while adding one narrow comment for Unk1PData.x4 as an EntryName slot.
Used exact subsystem includes for newly referenced APIs instead of relying on incidental declarations.
decomp_lessons: When matching game-mode code, byte-vs-halfword field widths can be decisive; update struct field sizes to match observed arithmetic and return casts instead of hiding width mismatches with casts.
Small table-management functions often reveal struct layout: gm_8017E280 and fn_8017E3C8 together imply lbl_804706D8 entries contain a signed/invalid key field and a byte-sized trophy unlock state.
Callback argument widths should be corrected at the type source. The StartMeleeRules.x50 change to u8 makes future callback implementations less likely to drift toward int-only prototypes.
For partially understood 1P/adventure structs, adding explicitly placed unknown byte fields is preferable to treating the whole area as padding when matched code reads those bytes individually.
Special-case character behavior, such as CKIND_GKOOPS clearing xC_b1, should be represented directly once observed rather than hidden behind an unknown helper.
naming_conventions: Unmatched or not-yet-understood functions retain address-based names such as fn_8017DD7C, gm_8017E280, and gm_8017E7A0.
Unknown struct fields continue to use offset-like names such as x0, x2, x3, x4, and x8 unless semantics are supported by evidence.
Nested unknown structs follow offset-derived names such as Unk1PData_x24.
Known enum and API names use project conventions such as CHKIND_NONE, CKIND_GKOOPS, Trophy_SetUnlockState, Player_LoseStock, and ifStatus_802F6948.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in fn_8017E3C8, indicating a stack-shape adjustment was needed for matching that otherwise simple loop.
Used explicit u8 casts and u8 return behavior in gm_8017E280, including returning -1 through an s8 function result when the fixed table has no free entry.
Used fixed loop bounds of 3 for companion Unk1PData_x24 entries and 12 for lbl_804706D8 entries, matching the apparent static array sizes.
Changed gm_8017E280's second parameter from bool to u32 even though the stored field is u8, preserving the apparent call/ABI type while narrowing only at storage/return points.
Matched simple control flow with early returns: gm_8017E4C4 returns on match or NULL at the sentinel, and gm_8017E440 returns fallback slot 4 on validation failure.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice. The context reports one issue comment but its body excerpt is empty, so reviewer intent or requested changes cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or further type Unk1PData_x24 fields x1, x2, x3, x4, and x8.
Search for additional users of lbl_804706D8 to confirm whether x2 is always trophy unlock state and whether x3 has a known role.
Find callers of gm_8017E280 to determine whether its u32 arg1 should eventually be narrowed or named based on trophy unlock semantics.
Check later changes to StartMeleeRules.x50 and gm_8017E7FC to confirm all match-end callbacks consistently use u8 arguments.
Search for gm_803DE650_t definitions or later table renames to identify the adventure lookup entries returned by gm_8017E4C4.
